replay.guidanceText:
CRITICAL RETRY GUIDANCE — OPENING PRESERVATION:
The previous attempt removed, obscured, cropped, or concealed an existing opening.
Preserve all windows and doors.
Do not place furniture, artwork, wall panels, decorative features, cabinetry, or staging elements in positions that conceal openings.
Ensure all original openings remain clearly visible and structurally unchanged.

CRITICAL RETRY GUIDANCE — FIXTURE PRESERVATION:
Preserve all existing built-in fixtures exactly as they appear in the original image.
Do not remove, replace, recolour, redesign, relocate, or materially alter cabinetry, countertops, sinks, appliances, fixed lighting, faucets, HVAC units, wardrobes, shelving, or other built-in fixtures.

CRITICAL RETRY GUIDANCE — ENVELOPE PRESERVATION:
Preserve all walls, ceilings, bulkheads, trims, architectural features, and room geometry exactly as they appear in the original image.

locations: window opening in the right side of the near wall

reasons: Gemini structure: Tier 1 structural violation: A window opening (right-hand side) has been removed and replaced by a continuous wall surface. This constitutes a change in the room envelope and wall structure.

locations: window opening in the right side of the right wall
window opening in the left side of the left wall

advisorySignals: envelope_confirmed_structural_change: A vertical wall recess visible on the left wall in the baseline image has been filled, creating a continuous flat wall surface in the staged image. This constitutes a structural modification of the architectural envelope.

reasons: Gemini structure: A window opening present in the BEFORE image has been removed and the wall infilled in the AFTER image, constituting a Tier 1 structural change., The wall structure has been altered by removing an architectural opening, violating the room's original geometry.

opening.explanation: The window opening in the right side of the right wall is not detectable in the AFTER image. The window opening in the left side of the left wall appears on a different wall than in the original image and appears materially different in size or shape.

floor.reason: The structural floor material is carpet in both images and remains unchanged. The rug in the staged image is a non-structural addition.

envelope.reason: The architectural envelope, including wall layout, window placement, and ceiling geometry, remains identical. The presence and location of windows and the single ceiling light fixture are consistent. Furniture and decor changes, along with the occlusion of the right-hand window by the bed, are permissible staging elements and do not constitute architectural alterations.
